name: AIDA Transition Index Standard
thesis: Stages are states. Value lives in transitions.

dimensions.name: Signal Quality
dimensions.meaning: Strength and meaning of the signal. Measures whether the observed behavior indicates genuine movement or only surface activity.

dimensions.name: Movement Probability
dimensions.meaning: Likelihood of transition. Estimates the likelihood that a prospect will move from the current state to the next state.

dimensions.name: Transition Velocity
dimensions.meaning: Speed and efficiency of movement. Velocity must be interpreted by context.

dimensions.name: Friction Resilience
dimensions.meaning: Ability to withstand obstacles. Measures how well the transition withstands or reduces obstacles that would otherwise prevent movement.

dimensions.name: Drop-Off Resilience
dimensions.meaning: Resistance to abandonment. Measures how well the transition resists abandonment before the next state is reached.

dimensions.name: Commercial Consequence
dimensions.meaning: Business value of the movement. ATI does not only measure movement; it measures movement that matters.

dimensions.name: Intervention Clarity
dimensions.meaning: Clarity of corrective action. Measures whether the cause of weakness is identifiable enough to suggest an action.

vector_weighting.declaration_rule: Any non-default weighting must be explicitly declared in the output.

diagnostic_classes.name: Transition Strong
diagnostic_classes.meaning: Movement is healthy, clear, measurable, and commercially useful.

diagnostic_classes.name: Transition Functional
diagnostic_classes.meaning: Movement exists and is generally healthy, but there are visible optimization gaps.
diagnostic_classes.action: Identify friction, improve velocity, and strengthen evidence.

diagnostic_classes.name: Transition Unstable
diagnostic_classes.meaning: Movement occurs inconsistently or depends on weak, fragmented, or unstable signals.
diagnostic_classes.action: Classify failure modes and prioritize intervention.

diagnostic_classes.name: Transition Constrained
diagnostic_classes.meaning: Movement is weak, delayed, or structurally blocked.
diagnostic_classes.action: Treat as a strategic funnel problem requiring focused correction.

diagnostic_classes.name: Transition Critical
diagnostic_classes.meaning: Movement is failing, unmeasured, or commercially ineffective.
diagnostic_classes.action: Diagnose immediately before scaling traffic, spend, or automation.

evidence_confidence.separation_rule: Evidence Confidence must be reported separately from ATI score. Evidence Confidence must never be mathematically included in ATI score.

levels.name: Absent Evidence
levels.output_treatment: Numeric score should not be issued.

levels.name: Self-Reported Evidence
levels.description: Based mainly on user answers or internal belief.

levels.name: Observable Evidence
levels.description: Based on visible metrics, behavior, or reported funnel data.

levels.name: Multi-Source Evidence
levels.description: Based on multiple signals across channels or systems.

levels.name: Validated Evidence
levels.description: Based on tracked, repeated, and verifiable data patterns.

e0_rule.statement: If Evidence Confidence is E0, ATI should not issue a numeric score.

partial_profile_rule.statement: If one or more ATI vectors are assigned E0 Absent Evidence, a clean composite ATI score must not be issued. A partial profile must be issued instead.
partial_profile_rule.basis: Operational extension of ATI Standard v1.1 E0 principle.
partial_profile_rule.correct_output_example: ATI Profile: Partial / Scorable vectors: T1, T2, T3 / Unscorable vector: T4 / E0 Absent Evidence
partial_profile_rule.incorrect_output_example: ATI Score: 61.5 (when any vector is E0)

governance_rules_summary: ATI must always score transitions, not stages.
Preference and intent remain sub-signals inside T2.
T4 remains Action to Loyalty and must not be removed.
Scanner outputs must map to T1-T4.
Failure modes must map to TFO.
Evidence Confidence must be reported separately from ATI score.
Evidence Confidence must never be mathematically included in ATI score.
E0 outputs must not issue numeric scores.
E1 outputs must use provisional language.
E2 outputs must use indicative language.
E3-E4 outputs may use stronger confidence language but must not imply guaranteed causality.
